topic: Philosophy of Religion: Self, Death & Afterlife
subject: religious-studies

items.q: Is resurrection the same as resuscitation of a corpse?
items.correct: No - in Christian teaching resurrection is transformation into glorified embodied life, as in Paul's 'spiritual body', not a mere return to ordinary mortal existence.
items.misconception: Resurrection just means a dead body coming back to its old life.
items.random_wrong: Resurrection means the soul becoming a ghost that haunts the earth.
items.why: Conflating resurrection with resuscitation misses the transformation claim that the doctrine actually makes and that philosophers debate.

items.q: Does Buddhism teach that a soul transmigrates from body to body?
items.correct: No - rebirth occurs without a permanent soul; continuity is likened to one flame lighting another, which distinguishes Buddhist rebirth from Hindu reincarnation of the atman.
items.misconception: Buddhism and Hinduism teach exactly the same view of an immortal soul moving between bodies.
items.random_wrong: Buddhism teaches that people are reborn only as animals.
items.why: The anatta doctrine is the key difference between the traditions, and exam answers regularly blur it.

items.q: Does being able to imagine leaving your body prove dualism?
items.correct: No - critics reply that conceivability does not establish real possibility, and materialists point to the close dependence of mind on brain as counter-evidence.
items.misconception: Because I can imagine existing without my body, the soul must exist.
items.random_wrong: Dualism was proven in laboratory experiments in the 1960s.
items.why: Descartes-style arguments need the contested step from imagination to possibility; spotting that gap is core AO2 material.

items.q: What did Descartes claim about mind and body?
items.correct: They are two distinct substances: a non-physical mind and a physical body
items.why: The answer examiners want is: They are two distinct substances: a non-physical mind and a physical body. “They are identical” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What does Hick's replica theory defend?
items.correct: The logical possibility that God re-creates an exact replica of the dead person in another space
items.misconception: The claim that souls sleep until judgement day
items.random_wrong: The view that clones share one soul
items.why: The answer examiners want is: The logical possibility that God re-creates an exact replica of the dead person in another space. “The claim that souls sleep until judgement day” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What does the Buddhist teaching of anatta claim?
items.correct: There is no permanent, unchanging self
items.misconception: The soul migrates unchanged between bodies
items.why: It's There is no permanent, unchanging self. “The soul migrates unchanged between bodies” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: What does a materialist typically conclude about death?
items.correct: Consciousness depends on the brain, so it ceases when the brain dies
items.misconception: The soul escapes the body at death
items.random_wrong: Death is an illusion of the senses
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Consciousness depends on the brain, so it ceases when the brain dies. “The soul escapes the body at death” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
